tif

Class IFDHandler

  • java.lang.Object
    • tif.IFDHandler
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ImageHandler, java.lang.AutoCloseable

    public class IFDHandler
extends java.lang.Object
implements ImageHandler
    This handler performs the heavy lifting of parsing TIFF-based metadata structures (Standard TIFF, EXIF in JPEGs, and DNG) by interpreting Image File Directories (IFDs).

    It performs a deep recursive traversal of the IFD chain, following pointers to sub-directories such as EXIF, GPS, and INTEROP. It adheres to the TIFF 6.0 specification for directory structures and tag entry parsing.

    Note: While this handler detects BigTIFF (version 43), it currently supports only Standard TIFF (version 42). This class focuses exclusively on IFD-based metadata.

    Version:
    1.5
    Author:
    Trevor Maggs

    • Constructor Detail

      • IFDHandler

        private IFDHandler(BinaryInput reader)
        Constructs a handler internally using an existing byte stream reader.
        Parameters:
        reader - the stream reader providing access to TIFF content

      • IFDHandler

        public IFDHandler(java.nio.file.Path fpath)
           throws java.io.IOException
        Constructs a handler that reads metadata from a file on disk.

        To ensure the underlying file handle is released and to prevent file locks, this handler should be managed within a try-with-resources block.

        Parameters:
        fpath - the path to the image file
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if the file is inaccessible

      • IFDHandler

        public IFDHandler(byte[] payload)
        Constructs a handler for in-memory byte arrays.
        Parameters:
        payload - byte array containing TIFF-formatted data

    • Method Detail

      • close

        public void close()
           throws java.io.IOException
        Closes the underlying stream reader and releases system resources.
        Specified by:
        close in interface java.lang.AutoCloseable
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException

      • getDirectories

        public java.util.List<DirectoryIFD> getDirectories()
        Returns the list of IFD directories that were successfully parsed.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable List of all parsed DirectoryIFD structures

      • getTifByteOrder

        public java.nio.ByteOrder getTifByteOrder()
        Returns the byte order, indicating how metadata values should be interpreted correctly.
        Returns:
        the ByteOrder detected in the TIFF header

      • isBigTiffVersion

        public boolean isBigTiffVersion()
        Indicates whether the parsed file is a BigTIFF variant (version 43).
        Returns:
        true if the file magic number identifies it as BigTIFF (version 43)

      • isDngVersion

        public boolean isDngVersion()
        Indicates whether the parsed file structure is an Adobe Digital Negative (DNG).
        Returns:
        true if a DNGVersion tag was found inside the primary IFD0 block

      • parseMetadata

        public boolean parseMetadata()
        Executes the parsing logic, performing a deep scan and populating the directory list.

        It validates the header and traverses the IFD chain. In specific cases, for example: JPEGs where the thumbnail IFD1 appears before the primary IFD0, the handler re-orders the results to ensure the primary image metadata is consistently at index 0.

        If any part of the directory structure is found to be corrupt, or if a low-level streaming error occurs, the dataset is cleared to maintain data integrity.

        Specified by:
        parseMetadata in interface ImageHandler
        Returns:
        true if at least one valid directory (IFD0) was extracted successfully, or false on any type of errors

      • readTifHeader

        private long readTifHeader()
                    throws java.io.IOException
        Identifies byte order, TIFF version, and returns the initial IFD offset.

        Requirement: The stream must be positioned at the TIFF magic bytes. Any preambles (HEIF/JPEG markers) must be skipped prior to calling this method.

        Returns:
        the absolute offset to IFD0, or 0L if the header is malformed or unsupported, for example: BigTIFF
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs

      • navigateImageFileDirectory

        private boolean navigateImageFileDirectory(DirectoryIdentifier dirType,
                                           long startOffset)
                                    throws java.io.IOException
        Recursively traverses a physical IFD and its linked sub-directories.

        Each IFD consists of a 2-byte entry count followed by a sequence of 12-byte entries. Only directories that belong to the main IFD chain (IFD0, IFD1, etc.) are followed by a trailing 4-byte pointer to the next directory in that chain.

        Parameters:
        dirType - the directory type currently being processed
        startOffset - the absolute file offset where the IFD begins
        Returns:
        true if the directory and all linked sub-directories were parsed successfully
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an I/O error occurs while reading the file

      • resolvePointerOffsets

        private long[] resolvePointerOffsets(DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD entry)
        Builds an array of branched out offsets referenced by a SubIFD pointer entry.

        According to the TIFF and DNG specifications, a SubIFDs pointer can reference either a single child directory or an array of child directory offsets. This method transparently decodes data in both layouts.

        If the payload is truncated or corrupted, any successfully decoded offsets are preserved with this condition logged.

        Parameters:
        entry - the DirectoryIFD.EntryIFD containing the SubIFD pointer
        Returns:
        an array of absolute file offsets identifying the start of each SubIFD, or an empty array if no valid offsets can be extracted

      • navigateImageFileDirectory2

        @Deprecated
private boolean navigateImageFileDirectory2(DirectoryIdentifier dirType,
                                                         long startOffset)
                                                  throws java.io.IOException
        Deprecated. 
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
